title: Resource Transport & Distribution

ItemBuffer.fields: long[] buffer (packed TimeItem: short data, short item, float time), int index
key_methods: accept(Item, short data) → buffer[index++] = TimeItem.get(data, item.id, Time.time)
poll(float speed) → returns item if Time.time >= buffer[0].time + speed (or time wrap); else null

DirectionalItemBuffer.fields: long[4][] buffers (4 directions × capacity), int[5] indexes
key_methods: accept(int dir, Item) → buffers[dir][indexes[dir]++] = BufferItem.get(item.id, Time.time)
poll(int dir, float speed) → returns item if time elapsed; else null
read(Reads, boolean legacy) → converts old 1-byte item format to 2-byte

Tiles.fields: Tile[] array, Puddle[] puddles, Fire[] fires, int width/height, long[] tmpFloorState/tmpBlockState
key_methods: fill() → populates with empty Tile objects

activity_flows.name: Conveyor Item Transport Loop
steps: IF len==0 AND timeScale==1 → set clogHeat=0, sleep(), return
Compute nextMax (aligned to next conveyor? 1 - max(itemSpace - nextc.minitem,0) : 1)
FOR each item i from back to front:
Compute maxmove = clamp(nextpos - ys[i], 0, moved)
ys[i] += maxmove; cap at nextMax
xs[i] approach 0 (centering)
IF ys[i] >= 1.0 → call pass(ids[i])
pass(): IF next != null AND next.acceptItem(this, item) → next.handleItem(this, item); remove item; return true
ELSE: item stays, waits
ELSE: track minitem
IF minitem < itemSpace threshold → increment clogHeat (max 1.0); ELSE clogHeat=0
acceptItem(source, item): checks len<capacity, direction angle (0=aligned, ±1=side), minitem spacing
handleItem(source, item): inserts at index 0 (front-facing) or mid (side-facing) with xs offset

activity_flows.name: Router Distribution (Round-Robin)
steps: IF lastItem==null AND items.any() → lastItem = items.first()
IF lastItem != null → time += 1/speed * delta()
Call getTileTarget(lastItem, lastInput, false) to find candidate
IF target found AND (time>=1 OR target is not Router/instantTransfer):
getTileTarget(lastItem, lastInput, true) — advance rotation counter
target.handleItem(this, lastItem)
items.remove(lastItem, 1); lastItem = null
getTileTarget(): iterate proximity[] starting at rotation offset;
SKIP tile that matches lastInput if source was overflowGate
RETURN first neighbor where acceptItem(this, item)==true
IF unit-controlled: use aim angle → compute quadrant → nearby(angle).acceptItem
acceptItem(): ONLY accept if lastItem==null AND items.total()==0 (capacity=1)
handleItem(): items.add, lastItem=item, time=0, lastInput=source.tile

activity_flows.name: Junction Crossover (Directional Buffer)
steps: FOR each direction i in [0..3]:
IF buffer.indexes[i] > 0:
Cap indexes[i] to capacity if overflowed
Read buffer[i][0]: extract time via BufferItem.time()
IF Time.time >= time + speed/timeScale OR time wrap → item is ready
dest = nearby(i)
IF item==null OR dest==null OR !dest.acceptItem(this,item) OR wrong team → SKIP (keep waiting)
ELSE → dest.handleItem(this, item); remove from buffer (arraycopy + indexes[i]--)
handleItem(source, item): relative = source.relativeTo(tile); buffer.accept(relative, item)
acceptItem(source, item): relative = source.relativeTo(tile); check buffer.accepts(relative) AND nearby(relative) exists

activity_flows.name: OverflowGate Routing (Forward-Priority with Fallback)
activity_flows.trigger: Instant transfer — no updateTile(); called synchronously on item arrival
steps: acceptItem(source, item): call getTileTarget(item, source, false); return non-null AND acceptItem AND same team
handleItem(source, item): call getTileTarget(item, source, true); IF target → target.handleItem(this, item)
getTileTarget(item, src, flip):
from = relativeToEdge(src.tile); IF -1 → null
to = nearby((from+2)%4)  [opposite = forward direction]
canForward = to exists AND !instantTransfer-to-instantTransfer AND to.acceptItem
inv = (invert == enabled)  [XOR flag]
IF canForward AND NOT inv → return forward 'to'
ELSE: try sides a=nearby((from-1)%4), b=nearby((from+1)%4)
IF neither side accepts: return (inv&&canForward ? to : null)
IF only a accepts → return a
IF only b accepts → return b
IF both accept → alternate via rotation bitmask; IF flip → toggle bit

activity_flows.name: OverflowDuct Transport (Timed with Overflow Fallback)
steps: progress += edelta()/speed*2
IF current != null AND progress >= (1 - 1/speed):
target = target()
IF target != null → target.handleItem(this, current); cdump toggle (0↔2); items.remove; current=null; progress %= threshold
IF current==null AND items.total()>0 → current = items.first()
target() when normal (not invert):
TRY front() first — forward direction
IF front blocked: iterate sides with cdump round-robin (dir = mod(rotation + offset + cdump, 4), skip rotation dir)
target() when invert:
TRY left() and right() first
IF both accept → use cdump to alternate
TRY front() as last resort
acceptItem(source, item): current==null AND items==0 AND source faces rotation direction

activity_flows.name: BufferedItemBridge Transport (Buffered Cross-Distance)
activity_flows.trigger: updateTransport(other: Building) called by parent ItemBridge.updateTile()
steps: IF buffer.accepts() AND items.total()>0 → item = items.take(); buffer.accept(item)
item = buffer.poll(speed / timeScale)  [speed-gated FIFO dequeue]
IF timer(timerAccept, 4/timeScale) fires AND item != null AND other.acceptItem(this, item):
moved = true
other.handleItem(this, item)
buffer.remove()
Serialization: buffer.write()/buffer.read() wraps ItemBuffer state

key_relationships: Router extends Block; RouterBuild implements ControlBlock
Tiles composes Tile[], Puddle[], Fire[] arrays
All Building subclasses call acceptItem()/handleItem() on neighbors via Building.nearby(dir)
important_notes: Common item-passing protocol: source calls dest.acceptItem(src, item) → if true → dest.handleItem(src, item)
ConveyorBuild tracks clogHeat [0..1]: rises when items are backed up (minitem < itemSpace); sleeping when empty
Router itemCapacity=1; only accepts when completely empty; round-robin via rotating 'rotation' counter mod proximity.size
Junction uses DirectionalItemBuffer per incoming direction; items cross without merging; speed=26 frames transit
OverflowGate is instantTransfer (synchronous, no tick); prefers forward, falls to alternating sides
OverflowDuct is a 1-item duct with progress timer; normal=forward-first, invert=sides-first
ItemBuffer packed format: long = TimeItem(short data, short item, float time); supports legacy 1-byte item read
Tiles.tmpFloorState / tmpBlockState are nullable lazy-init long arrays for transient state
Conveyor aligns xs of items when passing to aligned next conveyor (same rotation)
Router skips overflowGate-sourced tiles during round-robin to prevent feedback loops
